message.content:
Implement EXACTLY task **T98** (goals:G4) end-to-end in your isolated worktree on branch `implement/T98`, base commit `9c793be05b05ed44a1bf9f86aee0f514d2de977b` (current main). If your worktree HEAD is not that commit, `git reset --hard 9c793be05b05ed44a1bf9f86aee0f514d2de977b` (sanctioned).

**Headline:** Sync AGENTS.md, design/install/README docs and example config for multi-peer

**Description:** Update docs in the same feature change (per AGENTS.md doc-sync rule): AGENTS.md invariants (A1 now literally one-virtual-endpoint-per-peer), docs/design.md (per-peer PSK enabler, authenticated path->peer demux, bootstrap/roaming/limits, threat model), docs/install.md + README.md (multi-peer concentrator operation, per-peer psk/name, single-peer back-compat), and wanbond.example.toml (a commented multi-peer stanza with per-peer psk+name). Explicitly document that the plural [[wireguard.peers]] concentrator schema is now supported.

**Acceptance:** Docs describe the per-peer model, the demux/threat model, and the single-peer back-compat rule; wanbond.example.toml contains a working multi-peer example that parses via the config test suite; `go build ./...` and the docs/link checks are unaffected; grep finds no stale claim that the concentrator supports only one peer.

This is a DOCS + example-config task (the multi-peer concentrator feature already landed across T80/T89–T97 — per-peer psk/name in config.Peer, authenticated demux in internal/bind/multipath.go, per-peer `peer` metrics label). GROUND every doc claim in the SHIPPED code — read internal/config (Peer.PSK/Name), internal/bind/multipath.go (peerBySource demux, PROBE binding), internal/metrics (per-peer label). Every wanbond.example.toml example MUST parse via `config.Load` — if a config test exists that reads the example file (e.g. TestExampleConfigLoads), extend it to cover your multi-peer stanza; otherwise ensure the example round-trips through Load. Do NOT invent config fields that don't exist in internal/config.

Gate (MUST be green before you commit): `nix develop -c sh -c 'go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...'` (go is NOT on bare PATH — use nix develop). Commit on `implement/T98`. Return the standard structured result JSON: {taskId, status, resultCommit, branch, filesTouched, checkSummary, summary}. Do NOT mutate the ledger, do NOT merge. End with a `### Session summary` block.

```go
704	// session id never conflates two peers' probe streams, and a runtime-added path (T30) reuses it
705	// so its probes join this boot's stream and the peer's reflector adopts them without a
706	// challenge reset.
707	func buildScheduler(cfg *config.Config, psk config.Key, sessionID uint64, lg log.Logger) (sched.Scheduler, []*telemetry.Prober, bind.ProberFactory, error) {
708		clock := telemetry.SystemClock{}
709		proberCfg := telemetry.ProberConfig{
710			LossWindow: telemetry.DefaultLossWindow,
711			Liveness: telemetry.LivenessConfig{
712				DownAfter:        telemetry.DefaultDownAfter,
713				UpAfterSuccesses: telemetry.DefaultUpSuccesses,
714			},
715		}
716		// newProber mints one path's Prober with the shared session/config/clock, keyed on THIS
717		// peer's psk. It is the single construction point for this peer's boot-time AND runtime
718		// paths, so both measure liveness identically.
719		newProber := func(name string, id uint8) *telemetry.Prober {
720			return telemetry.NewProber(name, id, sessionID, psk, proberCfg, clock, lg)
721		}
722		probers := make([]*telemetry.Prober, len(cfg.Paths))
723		health := make([]sched.PathHealth, len(cfg.Paths))
724		quality := make([]sched.PathQuality, len(cfg.Paths))
725		for i := range cfg.Paths {
726			probers[i] = newProber(cfg.Paths[i].Name, uint8(i))
727			health[i] = probers[i]
728			quality[i] = probers[i]
729		}
730		// Policy is a config choice: active-backup (P1, default) or the T21 weighted-
731		// aggregation policy. Both consume the SAME per-path *Prober set — a *Prober
732		// satisfies BOTH PathHealth (liveness) and PathQuality (RTT/loss Estimate) — so the
733		// probe transport drives the very liveness/quality the scheduler selects on, and the
734		// swap is behind config with no Bind change.
735		scheduler, err := selectScheduler(cfg, health, quality, clock, lg)
736		if err != nil {
737			return nil, nil, nil, err
738		}
739		return scheduler, probers, newProber, nil
740	}
741	
742	// selectScheduler builds the send scheduler the configured policy names, over the
743	// per-path health (and, for the weighted policy, quality) sources. active-backup is
744	// the P1 default; weighted is T21. The weighted knobs are validated at config load,
745	// so translating them here cannot fail on range — only NewWeighted's structural
746	// checks (which the wiring satisfies) apply.
747	func selectScheduler(cfg *config.Config, health []sched.PathHealth, quality []sched.PathQuality, clock telemetry.Clock, lg log.Logger) (sched.Scheduler, error) {
748		switch cfg.Scheduler.Policy {
749		case config.PolicyWeighted:
750			sc := cfg.Scheduler
751			return sched.NewWeighted(health, quality, sched.WeightedConfig{
752				PerPathCapacity:   sc.PerPathCapacityFPS,
753				EngageFraction:    sc.EngageFraction,
754				DisengageFraction: sc.DisengageFraction,
755				CollapseDwell:     sc.CollapseDwell,
756				LoadTau:           sc.LoadTau,
757				Pacing:            sc.PacingEnabled,
758				PacingBurst:       sc.PacingBurstFrames,
759				WeightRTTFloor:    sc.WeightRTTFloor,
760				WeightLossFloor:   sc.WeightLossFloor,
761			}, clock, lg)
762		default:
763			// active-backup (and the empty default, normalized to it at config load).
764			return sched.NewActiveBackup(
765				health,
766				sched.Config{FailbackAfter: defaultFailbackDwell},
767				clock,
768				lg,
769			)
770		}
771	}

781	// Bind. Idempotent. It also releases this tunnel's hold on the single-amnezia-
782	// engine-per-process guard exactly once, so a later Up may reconfigure the
783	// process-global amnezia state. Probing is stopped BEFORE the engine tears the
784	// bind's sockets down so no emission races the close.
785	func (t *Tunnel) Close() {

787		// the engine tears its sockets down. reloadMu serializes this against a concurrent
788		// SIGHUP-driven rebind.
789		t.reloadMu.Lock()
790		t.stopMetricsLocked()
791		t.reloadMu.Unlock()
792		if t.stopProbes != nil {
793			t.stopProbes()
794		}

796			t.stopReconcile()
797		}
798		if t.stopHubFailover != nil {

801		// Stop the DNS re-resolution loop between hub-failover and the engine teardown (T74): no
802		// re-resolve may race the engine peer's install/repoint or the socket close.
803		if t.stopResolution != nil {
804			t.stopResolution()
805		}
806		// Stop the WG-session monitor before the engine teardown (I2): no IpcGet may race the
807		// engine's Close.
808		if t.stopSession != nil {
809			t.stopSession()
810		}
811		t.dev.Close()
812		t.releaseOnce.Do(func() { globalAmneziaGuard.release(t.amnezia) })
813	}
```

**`internal/adaptivefec/config.go`** — Config(struct), Validate(method), MaxShards(constant), DefaultConfig(function)

```go
1	package adaptivefec
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"math"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// MaxShards mirrors the Reed-Solomon field limit enforced in internal/fec
10	// (maxShards) and restated in internal/config (maxFECShards): a coding group
11	// carries at most 256 shards (data + parity) total over GF(2^8). It is mirrored
12	// here — with this cross-reference rather than an import — so the controller can
13	// fail-fast on an out-of-field parity bound without coupling to the datapath.
14	const MaxShards = 256
15	
16	// Documented default control-law constants. They are the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
17	// for the tuning the simulation harness exercises; DefaultConfig assembles them.
18	// Every value is named and justified rather than appearing as a magic number in
19	// the algorithm.
20	const (
21		// DefaultDataShards is K, the fixed number of data shards per coding group the
22		// controller sizes parity for. Chosen to match a typical FEC group.
23		DefaultDataShards = 10
24	
25		// DefaultMaxParity caps M at 100% overhead (M == K): beyond a 1:1 parity ratio
26		// the marginal recovery per byte of overhead is poor, so the loop saturates
27		// there rather than spending unbounded bandwidth under pathological loss.
28		DefaultMaxParity = 10
29	
30		// DefaultAlpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample. 0.3 keeps ~3-4
31		// samples of memory: heavy enough to reject single-sample telemetry noise, light
32		// enough to react to a genuine sustained shift within a few probe intervals.
33		DefaultAlpha = 0.3
34	
35		// DefaultRaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (5%) at/above which the loop
36		// starts adding parity. Below it, occasional loss is cheaper to absorb via
37		// retransmit/latency than to mask with standing redundancy.
38		DefaultRaiseThreshold = 0.05
39	
40		// DefaultLowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (2%) at/below which the loop
41		// is eligible to shed parity. The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis
42		// deadband: loss wandering between 2% and 5% neither raises nor lowers M.
43		DefaultLowerThreshold = 0.02
44	
45		// DefaultSafetyFactor is the headroom over the mean loss the parity is sized to
46		// mask. 1.5 sizes each group to tolerate 1.5x the smoothed loss, covering the
47		// binomial variance by which an individual group exceeds the mean. It applies
48		// only when TargetResidual is unset (the legacy SafetyFactor sizing mode).
49		DefaultSafetyFactor = 1.5
50	
51		// DefaultMaxStep bounds |ΔM| per RateInterval. A single spike can move M by at
52		// most 2 parity shards per interval, so redundancy slews rather than jumps.
53		DefaultMaxStep = 2
54	
55		// DefaultRateInterval is the minimum wall time between two M changes. Combined
56		// with MaxStep it caps the redundancy slew rate at MaxStep shards per 500ms.
57		DefaultRateInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
58	
59		// DefaultDwell is how long smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold before
60		// the first decrease. 3s of quiet is required to shed parity, so a brief lull in
61		// a lossy period does not prematurely strip protection (raise fast, lower slow).
62		DefaultDwell = 3 * time.Second
63	)
64	
65	// Config parameterizes the adaptive FEC controller. All fields are validated at
66	// construction (fail-fast): a mis-tuned band or an out-of-field parity bound is
67	// rejected by NewController rather than producing undefined control behavior at
68	// runtime.
69	type Config struct {
70		// DataShards is K, the fixed group size the controller sizes parity for. >= 1.
71		DataShards int
72		// MaxParity is the upper bound on the emitted M. >= 1 and DataShards+MaxParity
73		// <= MaxShards (the Reed-Solomon field limit).
74		MaxParity int
75		// Alpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample, in (0,1]. Smaller = more
76		// smoothing.
77		Alpha float64

89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)

105	// DefaultConfig returns a Config populated from the documented default control
106	// constants.
107	func DefaultConfig() Config {
108		return Config{
109			DataShards:     DefaultDataShards,
110			MaxParity:      DefaultMaxParity,
111			Alpha:          DefaultAlpha,
112			RaiseThreshold: DefaultRaiseThreshold,
113			LowerThreshold: DefaultLowerThreshold,
114			SafetyFactor:   DefaultSafetyFactor,
115			MaxStep:        DefaultMaxStep,
116			RateInterval:   DefaultRateInterval,
117			Dwell:          DefaultDwell,
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Validate enforces the control-law invariants, failing fast so a mis-tuned
122	// controller is rejected at construction rather than misbehaving at runtime.
123	func (c Config) Validate() error {
124		if c.DataShards < 1 {
125			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards (K) must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
126		}
127		if c.MaxParity < 1 {
128			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxParity must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxParity)
129		}
130		if c.DataShards+c.MaxParity > MaxShards {
131			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards+MaxParity must be <= %d (Reed-Solomon field limit), got %d", MaxShards, c.DataShards+c.MaxParity)
132		}
133		if !(c.Alpha > 0 && c.Alpha <= 1) || math.IsNaN(c.Alpha) {
134			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Alpha must be in (0,1], got %g", c.Alpha)
135		}
136		if math.IsNaN(c.LowerThreshold) || c.LowerThreshold < 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold must be >= 0, got %g", c.LowerThreshold)
138		}
139		if math.IsNaN(c.RaiseThreshold) || c.RaiseThreshold >= 1 {
140			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RaiseThreshold must be < 1, got %g", c.RaiseThreshold)
141		}
142		if c.LowerThreshold >= c.RaiseThreshold {
143			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold (%g) must be < RaiseThreshold (%g) so the hysteresis deadband is non-empty", c.LowerThreshold, c.RaiseThreshold)
144		}
145		if math.IsNaN(c.TargetResidual) {
146			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be a number, got NaN")
147		}
148		if c.TargetResidual != 0 {
149			// Residual-SLA sizing mode: TargetResidual governs and SafetyFactor is inert,
150			// so the two must not both be set (mutually exclusive, fail-fast).
151			if c.TargetResidual < 0 || c.TargetResidual >= 1 {
152				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be in (0,1) when set, got %g", c.TargetResidual)
153			}
154			if c.SafetyFactor != 0 {
155				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor and TargetResidual are mutually exclusive (TargetResidual is the primary residual-SLA surface); leave SafetyFactor 0 when TargetResidual is set, got SafetyFactor=%g", c.SafetyFactor)
156			}
157		} else if math.IsNaN(c.SafetyFactor) || math.IsInf(c.SafetyFactor, 0) || c.SafetyFactor < 1 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
```

```go
704	// session id never conflates two peers' probe streams, and a runtime-added path (T30) reuses it

707	func buildScheduler(cfg *config.Config, psk config.Key, sessionID uint64, lg log.Logger) (sched.Scheduler, []*telemetry.Prober, bind.ProberFactory, error) {
708		clock := telemetry.SystemClock{}
709		proberCfg := telemetry.ProberConfig{
710			LossWindow: telemetry.DefaultLossWindow,
711			Liveness: telemetry.LivenessConfig{
712				DownAfter:        telemetry.DefaultDownAfter,
713				UpAfterSuccesses: telemetry.DefaultUpSuccesses,
714			},
715		}
716		// newProber mints one path's Prober with the shared session/config/clock, keyed on THIS
717		// peer's psk. It is the single construction point for this peer's boot-time AND runtime
718		// paths, so both measure liveness identically.
719		newProber := func(name string, id uint8) *telemetry.Prober {
720			return telemetry.NewProber(name, id, sessionID, psk, proberCfg, clock, lg)
721		}
722		probers := make([]*telemetry.Prober, len(cfg.Paths))
723		health := make([]sched.PathHealth, len(cfg.Paths))
724		quality := make([]sched.PathQuality, len(cfg.Paths))
725		for i := range cfg.Paths {
726			probers[i] = newProber(cfg.Paths[i].Name, uint8(i))
727			health[i] = probers[i]
728			quality[i] = probers[i]
729		}
730		// Policy is a config choice: active-backup (P1, default) or the T21 weighted-
731		// aggregation policy. Both consume the SAME per-path *Prober set — a *Prober
732		// satisfies BOTH PathHealth (liveness) and PathQuality (RTT/loss Estimate) — so the
733		// probe transport drives the very liveness/quality the scheduler selects on, and the
734		// swap is behind config with no Bind change.
735		scheduler, err := selectScheduler(cfg, health, quality, clock, lg)
736		if err != nil {
737			return nil, nil, nil, err
738		}
739		return scheduler, probers, newProber, nil
740	}

742	// selectScheduler builds the send scheduler the configured policy names, over the
743	// per-path health (and, for the weighted policy, quality) sources. active-backup is
744	// the P1 default; weighted is T21. The weighted knobs are validated at config load,
745	// so translating them here cannot fail on range — only NewWeighted's structural
746	// checks (which the wiring satisfies) apply.
747	func selectScheduler(cfg *config.Config, health []sched.PathHealth, quality []sched.PathQuality, clock telemetry.Clock, lg log.Logger) (sched.Scheduler, error) {
748		switch cfg.Scheduler.Policy {

760				WeightLossFloor:   sc.WeightLossFloor,
761			}, clock, lg)
762		default:

764			return sched.NewActiveBackup(
765				health,
766				sched.Config{FailbackAfter: defaultFailbackDwell},
767				clock,
768				lg,
769			)
770		}

782	// engine-per-process guard exactly once, so a later Up may reconfigure the

787		// the engine tears its sockets down. reloadMu serializes this against a concurrent
788		// SIGHUP-driven rebind.
789		t.reloadMu.Lock()
790		t.stopMetricsLocked()
791		t.reloadMu.Unlock()
792		if t.stopProbes != nil {

794		}

797		}
798		if t.stopHubFailover != nil {

800		}
801		// Stop the DNS re-resolution loop between hub-failover and the engine teardown (T74): no
802		// re-resolve may race the engine peer's install/repoint or the socket close.
803		if t.stopResolution != nil {

805		}
806		// Stop the WG-session monitor before the engine teardown (I2): no IpcGet may race the
807		// engine's Close.
808		if t.stopSession != nil {
809			t.stopSession()
810		}
811		t.dev.Close()
812		t.releaseOnce.Do(func() { globalAmneziaGuard.release(t.amnezia) })
```

```go
9	// MaxShards mirrors the Reed-Solomon field limit enforced in internal/fec
10	// (maxShards) and restated in internal/config (maxFECShards): a coding group
11	// carries at most 256 shards (data + parity) total over GF(2^8). It is mirrored
12	// here — with this cross-reference rather than an import — so the controller can
13	// fail-fast on an out-of-field parity bound without coupling to the datapath.
14	const MaxShards = 256

16	// Documented default control-law constants. They are the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
17	// for the tuning the simulation harness exercises; DefaultConfig assembles them.
18	// Every value is named and justified rather than appearing as a magic number in
19	// the algorithm.

21		// DefaultDataShards is K, the fixed number of data shards per coding group the
22		// controller sizes parity for. Chosen to match a typical FEC group.
23		DefaultDataShards = 10
24	
25		// DefaultMaxParity caps M at 100% overhead (M == K): beyond a 1:1 parity ratio
26		// the marginal recovery per byte of overhead is poor, so the loop saturates
27		// there rather than spending unbounded bandwidth under pathological loss.
28		DefaultMaxParity = 10
29	
30		// DefaultAlpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample. 0.3 keeps ~3-4
31		// samples of memory: heavy enough to reject single-sample telemetry noise, light
32		// enough to react to a genuine sustained shift within a few probe intervals.
33		DefaultAlpha = 0.3
34	
35		// DefaultRaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (5%) at/above which the loop
36		// starts adding parity. Below it, occasional loss is cheaper to absorb via
37		// retransmit/latency than to mask with standing redundancy.
38		DefaultRaiseThreshold = 0.05
39	
40		// DefaultLowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (2%) at/below which the loop
41		// is eligible to shed parity. The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis
42		// deadband: loss wandering between 2% and 5% neither raises nor lowers M.
43		DefaultLowerThreshold = 0.02
44	
45		// DefaultSafetyFactor is the headroom over the mean loss the parity is sized to
46		// mask. 1.5 sizes each group to tolerate 1.5x the smoothed loss, covering the
47		// binomial variance by which an individual group exceeds the mean. It applies
48		// only when TargetResidual is unset (the legacy SafetyFactor sizing mode).
49		DefaultSafetyFactor = 1.5
50	
51		// DefaultMaxStep bounds |ΔM| per RateInterval. A single spike can move M by at
52		// most 2 parity shards per interval, so redundancy slews rather than jumps.
53		DefaultMaxStep = 2
54	
55		// DefaultRateInterval is the minimum wall time between two M changes. Combined
56		// with MaxStep it caps the redundancy slew rate at MaxStep shards per 500ms.
57		DefaultRateInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
58	
59		// DefaultDwell is how long smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold before
60		// the first decrease. 3s of quiet is required to shed parity, so a brief lull in
61		// a lossy period does not prematurely strip protection (raise fast, lower slow).
62		DefaultDwell = 3 * time.Second

88		// TargetResidual, when set (> 0), selects the RESIDUAL-SLA sizing mode and is
89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)
94		// when set; 0 selects the legacy SafetyFactor path.

158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
```

```go
718		// paths, so both measure liveness identically.
```

```go
1	package adaptivefec
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"math"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// MaxShards mirrors the Reed-Solomon field limit enforced in internal/fec
10	// (maxShards) and restated in internal/config (maxFECShards): a coding group
11	// carries at most 256 shards (data + parity) total over GF(2^8). It is mirrored
12	// here — with this cross-reference rather than an import — so the controller can
13	// fail-fast on an out-of-field parity bound without coupling to the datapath.
14	const MaxShards = 256
15	
16	// Documented default control-law constants. They are the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
17	// for the tuning the simulation harness exercises; DefaultConfig assembles them.
18	// Every value is named and justified rather than appearing as a magic number in
19	// the algorithm.
20	const (
21		// DefaultDataShards is K, the fixed number of data shards per coding group the
22		// controller sizes parity for. Chosen to match a typical FEC group.
23		DefaultDataShards = 10
24	
25		// DefaultMaxParity caps M at 100% overhead (M == K): beyond a 1:1 parity ratio
26		// the marginal recovery per byte of overhead is poor, so the loop saturates
27		// there rather than spending unbounded bandwidth under pathological loss.
28		DefaultMaxParity = 10
29	
30		// DefaultAlpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample. 0.3 keeps ~3-4
31		// samples of memory: heavy enough to reject single-sample telemetry noise, light
32		// enough to react to a genuine sustained shift within a few probe intervals.
33		DefaultAlpha = 0.3
34	
35		// DefaultRaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (5%) at/above which the loop
36		// starts adding parity. Below it, occasional loss is cheaper to absorb via
37		// retransmit/latency than to mask with standing redundancy.
38		DefaultRaiseThreshold = 0.05
39	
40		// DefaultLowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (2%) at/below which the loop
41		// is eligible to shed parity. The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis
42		// deadband: loss wandering between 2% and 5% neither raises nor lowers M.
43		DefaultLowerThreshold = 0.02
44	
45		// DefaultSafetyFactor is the headroom over the mean loss the parity is sized to
46		// mask. 1.5 sizes each group to tolerate 1.5x the smoothed loss, covering the
47		// binomial variance by which an individual group exceeds the mean. It applies
48		// only when TargetResidual is unset (the legacy SafetyFactor sizing mode).
49		DefaultSafetyFactor = 1.5
50	
51		// DefaultMaxStep bounds |ΔM| per RateInterval. A single spike can move M by at
52		// most 2 parity shards per interval, so redundancy slews rather than jumps.
53		DefaultMaxStep = 2
54	
55		// DefaultRateInterval is the minimum wall time between two M changes. Combined
56		// with MaxStep it caps the redundancy slew rate at MaxStep shards per 500ms.
57		DefaultRateInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
58	
59		// DefaultDwell is how long smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold before
60		// the first decrease. 3s of quiet is required to shed parity, so a brief lull in
61		// a lossy period does not prematurely strip protection (raise fast, lower slow).
62		DefaultDwell = 3 * time.Second
63	)
64	
65	// Config parameterizes the adaptive FEC controller. All fields are validated at
66	// construction (fail-fast): a mis-tuned band or an out-of-field parity bound is
67	// rejected by NewController rather than producing undefined control behavior at
68	// runtime.
69	type Config struct {
70		// DataShards is K, the fixed group size the controller sizes parity for. >= 1.
71		DataShards int
72		// MaxParity is the upper bound on the emitted M. >= 1 and DataShards+MaxParity
73		// <= MaxShards (the Reed-Solomon field limit).
74		MaxParity int
75		// Alpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample, in (0,1]. Smaller = more
76		// smoothing.
77		Alpha float64
78		// RaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/above which M may rise. In
79		// (LowerThreshold, 1).
80		RaiseThreshold float64
81		// LowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/below which M may fall. In
82		// [0, RaiseThreshold). The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis deadband.
83		LowerThreshold float64
84		// SafetyFactor is the headroom multiplier on the smoothed loss the parity is
85		// sized to mask. >= 1 in the SafetyFactor sizing mode (TargetResidual unset);
86		// MUST be 0 when TargetResidual is set (the two modes are mutually exclusive).
87		SafetyFactor float64
88		// TargetResidual, when set (> 0), selects the RESIDUAL-SLA sizing mode and is
89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)
94		// when set; 0 selects the legacy SafetyFactor path.
95		TargetResidual float64
96		// MaxStep bounds |ΔM| applied per RateInterval. >= 1.
97		MaxStep int
98		// RateInterval is the minimum time between two M changes. > 0.
99		RateInterval time.Duration
100		// Dwell is the minimum time smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold
101		// before the first decrease. >= 0.
102		Dwell time.Duration
103	}
104	
105	// DefaultConfig returns a Config populated from the documented default control
106	// constants.
107	func DefaultConfig() Config {
108		return Config{
109			DataShards:     DefaultDataShards,
110			MaxParity:      DefaultMaxParity,
111			Alpha:          DefaultAlpha,
112			RaiseThreshold: DefaultRaiseThreshold,
113			LowerThreshold: DefaultLowerThreshold,
114			SafetyFactor:   DefaultSafetyFactor,
115			MaxStep:        DefaultMaxStep,
116			RateInterval:   DefaultRateInterval,
117			Dwell:          DefaultDwell,
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Validate enforces the control-law invariants, failing fast so a mis-tuned
122	// controller is rejected at construction rather than misbehaving at runtime.
123	func (c Config) Validate() error {
124		if c.DataShards < 1 {
125			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards (K) must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
126		}
127		if c.MaxParity < 1 {
128			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxParity must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxParity)
129		}
130		if c.DataShards+c.MaxParity > MaxShards {
131			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards+MaxParity must be <= %d (Reed-Solomon field limit), got %d", MaxShards, c.DataShards+c.MaxParity)
132		}
133		if !(c.Alpha > 0 && c.Alpha <= 1) || math.IsNaN(c.Alpha) {
134			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Alpha must be in (0,1], got %g", c.Alpha)
135		}
136		if math.IsNaN(c.LowerThreshold) || c.LowerThreshold < 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold must be >= 0, got %g", c.LowerThreshold)
138		}
139		if math.IsNaN(c.RaiseThreshold) || c.RaiseThreshold >= 1 {
140			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RaiseThreshold must be < 1, got %g", c.RaiseThreshold)
141		}
142		if c.LowerThreshold >= c.RaiseThreshold {
143			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold (%g) must be < RaiseThreshold (%g) so the hysteresis deadband is non-empty", c.LowerThreshold, c.RaiseThreshold)
144		}
145		if math.IsNaN(c.TargetResidual) {
146			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be a number, got NaN")
147		}
148		if c.TargetResidual != 0 {
149			// Residual-SLA sizing mode: TargetResidual governs and SafetyFactor is inert,
150			// so the two must not both be set (mutually exclusive, fail-fast).
151			if c.TargetResidual < 0 || c.TargetResidual >= 1 {
152				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be in (0,1) when set, got %g", c.TargetResidual)
153			}
154			if c.SafetyFactor != 0 {
155				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor and TargetResidual are mutually exclusive (TargetResidual is the primary residual-SLA surface); leave SafetyFactor 0 when TargetResidual is set, got SafetyFactor=%g", c.SafetyFactor)
156			}
157		} else if math.IsNaN(c.SafetyFactor) || math.IsInf(c.SafetyFactor, 0) || c.SafetyFactor < 1 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
```

```go
1	package fec
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/binary"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// maxShards is the Reed-Solomon field limit: RS over GF(2^8) admits at most 256
10	// shards total (data + parity) per coding group.
11	const maxShards = 256
12	
13	// lenPrefixLen is the width of the big-endian length header prepended to each
14	// data payload before it is padded to the group's uniform shard length. It lets
15	// the decoder recover the exact original byte length after Reed-Solomon
16	// reconstruction, since RS operates only on fixed-length shards. A uint32 prefix
17	// covers any datagram this codec will ever wrap.
18	const lenPrefixLen = 4
19	
20	// ShardFramingOverhead is the fixed per-shard framing this codec adds on top of the
21	// caller's payload before Reed-Solomon coding — the big-endian length prefix. A
22	// parity shard's wire payload is ShardFramingOverhead + the group's largest coded
23	// data payload, so the multipath Bind uses it (with the outer parity/data header
24	// delta) to size the FEC MTU penalty (T24).
25	const ShardFramingOverhead = lenPrefixLen
26	
27	// Config parameterizes a Reed-Solomon FEC group: DataShards (N) opaque DATA
28	// frames are grouped and protected by ParityShards (K) parity frames, so the
29	// receiver recovers up to K arbitrary losses per group. Deadline bounds grouping
30	// latency: a group that does not fill to N within Deadline is flushed early over
31	// however many data frames it holds, so the parity is never delayed unboundedly
32	// waiting for a group to fill.
33	type Config struct {
34		// DataShards is N, the maximum number of DATA frames grouped before parity is
35		// emitted. Must be >= 1.
36		DataShards int
37		// ParityShards is K, the number of parity frames emitted per group and the
38		// maximum number of per-group losses the receiver can recover. Must be >= 1.
39		ParityShards int
40		// Deadline bounds the grouping latency: an open, partially-filled group is
41		// flushed (parity emitted over its current data frames) once this much time
42		// has elapsed since its first frame. Must be > 0.
43		Deadline time.Duration
44	}
45	
46	// Validate reports whether the configuration is a usable Reed-Solomon FEC ratio,
47	// failing fast on an out-of-range shard count or a non-positive deadline. It is the
48	// exported form of the same invariant NewEncoder/NewDecoder enforce, so a caller (the
49	// multipath Bind, T24) can reject a bad ratio before constructing either.
50	func (c Config) Validate() error { return c.validate() }
51	
52	func (c Config) validate() error {
53		if c.DataShards < 1 {
54			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
55		}
56		if c.ParityShards < 1 {
57			return fmt.Errorf("fec: ParityShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.ParityShards)
58		}
59		if c.DataShards+c.ParityShards > maxShards {
60			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards+ParityShards must be <= %d, got %d", maxShards, c.DataShards+c.ParityShards)
61		}
62		if c.Deadline <= 0 {
63			return fmt.Errorf("fec: Deadline must be > 0, got %s", c.Deadline)
64		}
65		return nil
66	}
67	
68	// GroupID identifies one FEC coding group. The encoder assigns group ids
69	// monotonically; the decoder buckets received shards by this id.
70	type GroupID uint32
71	
72	// Shard is the closed sum over the two kinds of frame an FEC group emits: a
73	// DataShard wrapping one opaque payload, and a ParityShard carrying redundancy.
74	// The unexported marker prevents other packages from introducing new kinds.
75	type Shard interface {
76		// GroupID returns the coding group this shard belongs to.
77		GroupID() GroupID
78		isShard()
79	}
80	
81	// DataShard is one opaque DATA payload admitted into a group, tagged with its
82	// coding coordinates so the decoder can place it. It carries no group cardinality
83	// (M), because a data frame is emitted immediately on admission — before the
84	// group's final size is known — to keep datapath latency low; the decoder learns
85	// M from any ParityShard of the group.
86	type DataShard struct {
87		Group   GroupID
88		Index   int    // position within the group, 0..M-1
89		Payload []byte // the opaque original bytes (never inspected)
90	}
91	
92	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
93	func (d DataShard) GroupID() GroupID { return d.Group }
94	func (DataShard) isShard()           {}
95	
96	// ParityShard is one Reed-Solomon parity frame for a group. It carries DataCount
97	// (M) and its Payload length equals the group's uniform shard length, so a single
98	// received parity frame tells the decoder both the group cardinality and the
99	// shard geometry it needs to reconstruct losses.
100	type ParityShard struct {
101		Group     GroupID
102		Index     int    // parity position, 0..K-1; RS shard index is DataCount+Index
103		DataCount int    // M: number of data shards this parity protects
104		Payload   []byte // parity bytes; len == group shard length
105	}
106	
107	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
108	func (p ParityShard) GroupID() GroupID { return p.Group }
109	func (ParityShard) isShard()           {}
110	
111	// Recovered is a data payload the decoder reconstructed from parity — one that
112	// was NOT directly received. Directly-received data frames are never echoed back
113	// as Recovered; the caller already holds them.
114	type Recovered struct {
115		Group   GroupID
116		Index   int
117		Payload []byte
118	}
119	
120	// encodeDataShard renders one payload as a fixed-length RS shard: a big-endian
121	// uint32 length prefix followed by the payload, right-padded with zeros to
122	// shardLen. shardLen is guaranteed >= lenPrefixLen+len(payload) by the caller.
123	func encodeDataShard(payload []byte, shardLen int) []byte {
124		s := make([]byte, shardLen)
125		binary.BigEndian.PutUint32(s, uint32(len(payload)))
126		copy(s[lenPrefixLen:], payload)
127		return s
128	}
129	
130	// decodeDataShard inverts encodeDataShard, returning the exact original payload.
131	// It fails fast on a length prefix that overruns the shard, which would indicate
132	// corruption or a decoder/encoder mismatch rather than a recoverable condition.
133	func decodeDataShard(shard []byte) ([]byte, error) {
134		if len(shard) < lenPrefixLen {
135			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard too short for length prefix: %d bytes", len(shard))
136		}
137		n := int(binary.BigEndian.Uint32(shard[:lenPrefixLen]))
138		if n > len(shard)-lenPrefixLen {
139			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard length prefix %d overruns shard payload %d", n, len(shard)-lenPrefixLen)
140		}
141		out := make([]byte, n)
142		copy(out, shard[lenPrefixLen:lenPrefixLen+n])
143		return out, nil
144	}
```

```go
1	package adaptivefec
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"

8	
9	// MaxShards mirrors the Reed-Solomon field limit enforced in internal/fec
10	// (maxShards) and restated in internal/config (maxFECShards): a coding group
11	// carries at most 256 shards (data + parity) total over GF(2^8). It is mirrored
12	// here — with this cross-reference rather than an import — so the controller can
13	// fail-fast on an out-of-field parity bound without coupling to the datapath.
14	const MaxShards = 256
15	
16	// Documented default control-law constants. They are the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
17	// for the tuning the simulation harness exercises; DefaultConfig assembles them.
18	// Every value is named and justified rather than appearing as a magic number in
19	// the algorithm.
20	const (
21		// DefaultDataShards is K, the fixed number of data shards per coding group the
22		// controller sizes parity for. Chosen to match a typical FEC group.
23		DefaultDataShards = 10
24	
25		// DefaultMaxParity caps M at 100% overhead (M == K): beyond a 1:1 parity ratio
26		// the marginal recovery per byte of overhead is poor, so the loop saturates
27		// there rather than spending unbounded bandwidth under pathological loss.
28		DefaultMaxParity = 10
29	
30		// DefaultAlpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample. 0.3 keeps ~3-4
31		// samples of memory: heavy enough to reject single-sample telemetry noise, light
32		// enough to react to a genuine sustained shift within a few probe intervals.
33		DefaultAlpha = 0.3
34	
35		// DefaultRaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (5%) at/above which the loop
36		// starts adding parity. Below it, occasional loss is cheaper to absorb via
37		// retransmit/latency than to mask with standing redundancy.
38		DefaultRaiseThreshold = 0.05
39	
40		// DefaultLowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (2%) at/below which the loop
41		// is eligible to shed parity. The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis
42		// deadband: loss wandering between 2% and 5% neither raises nor lowers M.
43		DefaultLowerThreshold = 0.02
44	
45		// DefaultSafetyFactor is the headroom over the mean loss the parity is sized to
46		// mask. 1.5 sizes each group to tolerate 1.5x the smoothed loss, covering the
47		// binomial variance by which an individual group exceeds the mean. It applies
48		// only when TargetResidual is unset (the legacy SafetyFactor sizing mode).
49		DefaultSafetyFactor = 1.5
50	
51		// DefaultMaxStep bounds |ΔM| per RateInterval. A single spike can move M by at
52		// most 2 parity shards per interval, so redundancy slews rather than jumps.
53		DefaultMaxStep = 2
54	
55		// DefaultRateInterval is the minimum wall time between two M changes. Combined
56		// with MaxStep it caps the redundancy slew rate at MaxStep shards per 500ms.
57		DefaultRateInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
58	
59		// DefaultDwell is how long smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold before
60		// the first decrease. 3s of quiet is required to shed parity, so a brief lull in
61		// a lossy period does not prematurely strip protection (raise fast, lower slow).
62		DefaultDwell = 3 * time.Second
63	)
64	
65	// Config parameterizes the adaptive FEC controller. All fields are validated at
66	// construction (fail-fast): a mis-tuned band or an out-of-field parity bound is
67	// rejected by NewController rather than producing undefined control behavior at
68	// runtime.
69	type Config struct {
70		// DataShards is K, the fixed group size the controller sizes parity for. >= 1.
71		DataShards int
72		// MaxParity is the upper bound on the emitted M. >= 1 and DataShards+MaxParity
73		// <= MaxShards (the Reed-Solomon field limit).
74		MaxParity int
75		// Alpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample, in (0,1]. Smaller = more
76		// smoothing.
77		Alpha float64
78		// RaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/above which M may rise. In
79		// (LowerThreshold, 1).
80		RaiseThreshold float64
81		// LowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/below which M may fall. In
82		// [0, RaiseThreshold). The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis deadband.
83		LowerThreshold float64
84		// SafetyFactor is the headroom multiplier on the smoothed loss the parity is
85		// sized to mask. >= 1 in the SafetyFactor sizing mode (TargetResidual unset);
86		// MUST be 0 when TargetResidual is set (the two modes are mutually exclusive).
87		SafetyFactor float64
88		// TargetResidual, when set (> 0), selects the RESIDUAL-SLA sizing mode and is
89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)
94		// when set; 0 selects the legacy SafetyFactor path.
95		TargetResidual float64
96		// MaxStep bounds |ΔM| applied per RateInterval. >= 1.
97		MaxStep int
98		// RateInterval is the minimum time between two M changes. > 0.
99		RateInterval time.Duration
100		// Dwell is the minimum time smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold
101		// before the first decrease. >= 0.
102		Dwell time.Duration
103	}
104	
105	// DefaultConfig returns a Config populated from the documented default control
106	// constants.
107	func DefaultConfig() Config {
108		return Config{
109			DataShards:     DefaultDataShards,
110			MaxParity:      DefaultMaxParity,
111			Alpha:          DefaultAlpha,
112			RaiseThreshold: DefaultRaiseThreshold,
113			LowerThreshold: DefaultLowerThreshold,
114			SafetyFactor:   DefaultSafetyFactor,
115			MaxStep:        DefaultMaxStep,
116			RateInterval:   DefaultRateInterval,
117			Dwell:          DefaultDwell,
118		}
119	}

121	// Validate enforces the control-law invariants, failing fast so a mis-tuned
122	// controller is rejected at construction rather than misbehaving at runtime.
123	func (c Config) Validate() error {
124		if c.DataShards < 1 {
125			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards (K) must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
126		}
127		if c.MaxParity < 1 {
128			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxParity must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxParity)
129		}
130		if c.DataShards+c.MaxParity > MaxShards {
131			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards+MaxParity must be <= %d (Reed-Solomon field limit), got %d", MaxShards, c.DataShards+c.MaxParity)
132		}
133		if !(c.Alpha > 0 && c.Alpha <= 1) || math.IsNaN(c.Alpha) {
134			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Alpha must be in (0,1], got %g", c.Alpha)
135		}
136		if math.IsNaN(c.LowerThreshold) || c.LowerThreshold < 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold must be >= 0, got %g", c.LowerThreshold)
138		}
139		if math.IsNaN(c.RaiseThreshold) || c.RaiseThreshold >= 1 {
140			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RaiseThreshold must be < 1, got %g", c.RaiseThreshold)
141		}
142		if c.LowerThreshold >= c.RaiseThreshold {
143			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold (%g) must be < RaiseThreshold (%g) so the hysteresis deadband is non-empty", c.LowerThreshold, c.RaiseThreshold)
144		}
145		if math.IsNaN(c.TargetResidual) {
146			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be a number, got NaN")
147		}
148		if c.TargetResidual != 0 {
149			// Residual-SLA sizing mode: TargetResidual governs and SafetyFactor is inert,
150			// so the two must not both be set (mutually exclusive, fail-fast).
151			if c.TargetResidual < 0 || c.TargetResidual >= 1 {
152				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be in (0,1) when set, got %g", c.TargetResidual)
153			}
154			if c.SafetyFactor != 0 {
155				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor and TargetResidual are mutually exclusive (TargetResidual is the primary residual-SLA surface); leave SafetyFactor 0 when TargetResidual is set, got SafetyFactor=%g", c.SafetyFactor)
156			}
157		} else if math.IsNaN(c.SafetyFactor) || math.IsInf(c.SafetyFactor, 0) || c.SafetyFactor < 1 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
```

```go
1	package fec
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/binary"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// maxShards is the Reed-Solomon field limit: RS over GF(2^8) admits at most 256
10	// shards total (data + parity) per coding group.
11	const maxShards = 256
12	
13	// lenPrefixLen is the width of the big-endian length header prepended to each
14	// data payload before it is padded to the group's uniform shard length. It lets
15	// the decoder recover the exact original byte length after Reed-Solomon
16	// reconstruction, since RS operates only on fixed-length shards. A uint32 prefix
17	// covers any datagram this codec will ever wrap.
18	const lenPrefixLen = 4
19	
20	// ShardFramingOverhead is the fixed per-shard framing this codec adds on top of the
21	// caller's payload before Reed-Solomon coding — the big-endian length prefix. A
22	// parity shard's wire payload is ShardFramingOverhead + the group's largest coded
23	// data payload, so the multipath Bind uses it (with the outer parity/data header
24	// delta) to size the FEC MTU penalty (T24).
25	const ShardFramingOverhead = lenPrefixLen
26	
27	// Config parameterizes a Reed-Solomon FEC group: DataShards (N) opaque DATA
28	// frames are grouped and protected by ParityShards (K) parity frames, so the
29	// receiver recovers up to K arbitrary losses per group. Deadline bounds grouping
30	// latency: a group that does not fill to N within Deadline is flushed early over
31	// however many data frames it holds, so the parity is never delayed unboundedly
32	// waiting for a group to fill.
33	type Config struct {
34		// DataShards is N, the maximum number of DATA frames grouped before parity is
35		// emitted. Must be >= 1.
36		DataShards int
37		// ParityShards is K, the number of parity frames emitted per group and the
38		// maximum number of per-group losses the receiver can recover. Must be >= 1.
39		ParityShards int
40		// Deadline bounds the grouping latency: an open, partially-filled group is
41		// flushed (parity emitted over its current data frames) once this much time
42		// has elapsed since its first frame. Must be > 0.
43		Deadline time.Duration
44	}
45	
46	// Validate reports whether the configuration is a usable Reed-Solomon FEC ratio,
47	// failing fast on an out-of-range shard count or a non-positive deadline. It is the
48	// exported form of the same invariant NewEncoder/NewDecoder enforce, so a caller (the
49	// multipath Bind, T24) can reject a bad ratio before constructing either.
50	func (c Config) Validate() error { return c.validate() }
51	
52	func (c Config) validate() error {
53		if c.DataShards < 1 {
54			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
55		}
56		if c.ParityShards < 1 {
57			return fmt.Errorf("fec: ParityShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.ParityShards)
58		}
59		if c.DataShards+c.ParityShards > maxShards {
60			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards+ParityShards must be <= %d, got %d", maxShards, c.DataShards+c.ParityShards)
61		}
62		if c.Deadline <= 0 {
63			return fmt.Errorf("fec: Deadline must be > 0, got %s", c.Deadline)
64		}
65		return nil
66	}
67	
68	// GroupID identifies one FEC coding group. The encoder assigns group ids
69	// monotonically; the decoder buckets received shards by this id.
70	type GroupID uint32
71	
72	// Shard is the closed sum over the two kinds of frame an FEC group emits: a
73	// DataShard wrapping one opaque payload, and a ParityShard carrying redundancy.
74	// The unexported marker prevents other packages from introducing new kinds.
75	type Shard interface {
76		// GroupID returns the coding group this shard belongs to.
77		GroupID() GroupID
78		isShard()
79	}
80	
81	// DataShard is one opaque DATA payload admitted into a group, tagged with its
82	// coding coordinates so the decoder can place it. It carries no group cardinality
83	// (M), because a data frame is emitted immediately on admission — before the
84	// group's final size is known — to keep datapath latency low; the decoder learns
85	// M from any ParityShard of the group.
86	type DataShard struct {
87		Group   GroupID
88		Index   int    // position within the group, 0..M-1
89		Payload []byte // the opaque original bytes (never inspected)
90	}
91	
92	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
93	func (d DataShard) GroupID() GroupID { return d.Group }
94	func (DataShard) isShard()           {}
95	
96	// ParityShard is one Reed-Solomon parity frame for a group. It carries DataCount
97	// (M) and its Payload length equals the group's uniform shard length, so a single
98	// received parity frame tells the decoder both the group cardinality and the
99	// shard geometry it needs to reconstruct losses.
100	type ParityShard struct {
101		Group     GroupID
102		Index     int    // parity position, 0..K-1; RS shard index is DataCount+Index
103		DataCount int    // M: number of data shards this parity protects
104		Payload   []byte // parity bytes; len == group shard length
105	}
106	
107	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
108	func (p ParityShard) GroupID() GroupID { return p.Group }
109	func (ParityShard) isShard()           {}
110	
111	// Recovered is a data payload the decoder reconstructed from parity — one that
112	// was NOT directly received. Directly-received data frames are never echoed back
113	// as Recovered; the caller already holds them.
114	type Recovered struct {
115		Group   GroupID
116		Index   int
117		Payload []byte
118	}
119	
120	// encodeDataShard renders one payload as a fixed-length RS shard: a big-endian
121	// uint32 length prefix followed by the payload, right-padded with zeros to
122	// shardLen. shardLen is guaranteed >= lenPrefixLen+len(payload) by the caller.
123	func encodeDataShard(payload []byte, shardLen int) []byte {
124		s := make([]byte, shardLen)
125		binary.BigEndian.PutUint32(s, uint32(len(payload)))
126		copy(s[lenPrefixLen:], payload)
127		return s
128	}
129	
130	// decodeDataShard inverts encodeDataShard, returning the exact original payload.
131	// It fails fast on a length prefix that overruns the shard, which would indicate
132	// corruption or a decoder/encoder mismatch rather than a recoverable condition.
133	func decodeDataShard(shard []byte) ([]byte, error) {
134		if len(shard) < lenPrefixLen {
135			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard too short for length prefix: %d bytes", len(shard))
136		}
137		n := int(binary.BigEndian.Uint32(shard[:lenPrefixLen]))
138		if n > len(shard)-lenPrefixLen {
139			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard length prefix %d overruns shard payload %d", n, len(shard)-lenPrefixLen)
140		}
141		out := make([]byte, n)
142		copy(out, shard[lenPrefixLen:lenPrefixLen+n])
143		return out, nil
144	}
```

```go
1	package adaptivefec
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"math"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// MaxShards mirrors the Reed-Solomon field limit enforced in internal/fec
10	// (maxShards) and restated in internal/config (maxFECShards): a coding group
11	// carries at most 256 shards (data + parity) total over GF(2^8). It is mirrored
12	// here — with this cross-reference rather than an import — so the controller can
13	// fail-fast on an out-of-field parity bound without coupling to the datapath.
14	const MaxShards = 256
15	
16	// Documented default control-law constants. They are the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
17	// for the tuning the simulation harness exercises; DefaultConfig assembles them.
18	// Every value is named and justified rather than appearing as a magic number in
19	// the algorithm.
20	const (
21		// DefaultDataShards is K, the fixed number of data shards per coding group the
22		// controller sizes parity for. Chosen to match a typical FEC group.
23		DefaultDataShards = 10
24	
25		// DefaultMaxParity caps M at 100% overhead (M == K): beyond a 1:1 parity ratio
26		// the marginal recovery per byte of overhead is poor, so the loop saturates
27		// there rather than spending unbounded bandwidth under pathological loss.
28		DefaultMaxParity = 10
29	
30		// DefaultAlpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample. 0.3 keeps ~3-4
31		// samples of memory: heavy enough to reject single-sample telemetry noise, light
32		// enough to react to a genuine sustained shift within a few probe intervals.
33		DefaultAlpha = 0.3
34	
35		// DefaultRaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (5%) at/above which the loop
36		// starts adding parity. Below it, occasional loss is cheaper to absorb via
37		// retransmit/latency than to mask with standing redundancy.
38		DefaultRaiseThreshold = 0.05
39	
40		// DefaultLowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (2%) at/below which the loop
41		// is eligible to shed parity. The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis
42		// deadband: loss wandering between 2% and 5% neither raises nor lowers M.
43		DefaultLowerThreshold = 0.02
44	
45		// DefaultSafetyFactor is the headroom over the mean loss the parity is sized to
46		// mask. 1.5 sizes each group to tolerate 1.5x the smoothed loss, covering the
47		// binomial variance by which an individual group exceeds the mean. It applies
48		// only when TargetResidual is unset (the legacy SafetyFactor sizing mode).
49		DefaultSafetyFactor = 1.5
50	
51		// DefaultMaxStep bounds |ΔM| per RateInterval. A single spike can move M by at
52		// most 2 parity shards per interval, so redundancy slews rather than jumps.
53		DefaultMaxStep = 2
54	
55		// DefaultRateInterval is the minimum wall time between two M changes. Combined
56		// with MaxStep it caps the redundancy slew rate at MaxStep shards per 500ms.
57		DefaultRateInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
58	
59		// DefaultDwell is how long smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold before
60		// the first decrease. 3s of quiet is required to shed parity, so a brief lull in
61		// a lossy period does not prematurely strip protection (raise fast, lower slow).
62		DefaultDwell = 3 * time.Second
63	)
64	
65	// Config parameterizes the adaptive FEC controller. All fields are validated at
66	// construction (fail-fast): a mis-tuned band or an out-of-field parity bound is
67	// rejected by NewController rather than producing undefined control behavior at
68	// runtime.
69	type Config struct {
70		// DataShards is K, the fixed group size the controller sizes parity for. >= 1.
71		DataShards int
72		// MaxParity is the upper bound on the emitted M. >= 1 and DataShards+MaxParity
73		// <= MaxShards (the Reed-Solomon field limit).
74		MaxParity int
75		// Alpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample, in (0,1]. Smaller = more
76		// smoothing.
77		Alpha float64
78		// RaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/above which M may rise. In
79		// (LowerThreshold, 1).
80		RaiseThreshold float64
81		// LowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/below which M may fall. In
82		// [0, RaiseThreshold). The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis deadband.
83		LowerThreshold float64
84		// SafetyFactor is the headroom multiplier on the smoothed loss the parity is
85		// sized to mask. >= 1 in the SafetyFactor sizing mode (TargetResidual unset);
86		// MUST be 0 when TargetResidual is set (the two modes are mutually exclusive).
87		SafetyFactor float64
88		// TargetResidual, when set (> 0), selects the RESIDUAL-SLA sizing mode and is
89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)
94		// when set; 0 selects the legacy SafetyFactor path.
95		TargetResidual float64
96		// MaxStep bounds |ΔM| applied per RateInterval. >= 1.
97		MaxStep int
98		// RateInterval is the minimum time between two M changes. > 0.
99		RateInterval time.Duration
100		// Dwell is the minimum time smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold
101		// before the first decrease. >= 0.
102		Dwell time.Duration
103	}
104	
105	// DefaultConfig returns a Config populated from the documented default control
106	// constants.
107	func DefaultConfig() Config {
108		return Config{
109			DataShards:     DefaultDataShards,
110			MaxParity:      DefaultMaxParity,
111			Alpha:          DefaultAlpha,
112			RaiseThreshold: DefaultRaiseThreshold,
113			LowerThreshold: DefaultLowerThreshold,
114			SafetyFactor:   DefaultSafetyFactor,
115			MaxStep:        DefaultMaxStep,
116			RateInterval:   DefaultRateInterval,
117			Dwell:          DefaultDwell,
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Validate enforces the control-law invariants, failing fast so a mis-tuned
122	// controller is rejected at construction rather than misbehaving at runtime.
123	func (c Config) Validate() error {
124		if c.DataShards < 1 {
125			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards (K) must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
126		}
127		if c.MaxParity < 1 {
128			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxParity must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxParity)
129		}
130		if c.DataShards+c.MaxParity > MaxShards {
131			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards+MaxParity must be <= %d (Reed-Solomon field limit), got %d", MaxShards, c.DataShards+c.MaxParity)
132		}
133		if !(c.Alpha > 0 && c.Alpha <= 1) || math.IsNaN(c.Alpha) {
134			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Alpha must be in (0,1], got %g", c.Alpha)
135		}
136		if math.IsNaN(c.LowerThreshold) || c.LowerThreshold < 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold must be >= 0, got %g", c.LowerThreshold)
138		}
139		if math.IsNaN(c.RaiseThreshold) || c.RaiseThreshold >= 1 {
140			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RaiseThreshold must be < 1, got %g", c.RaiseThreshold)
141		}
142		if c.LowerThreshold >= c.RaiseThreshold {
143			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold (%g) must be < RaiseThreshold (%g) so the hysteresis deadband is non-empty", c.LowerThreshold, c.RaiseThreshold)
144		}
145		if math.IsNaN(c.TargetResidual) {
146			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be a number, got NaN")
147		}
148		if c.TargetResidual != 0 {
149			// Residual-SLA sizing mode: TargetResidual governs and SafetyFactor is inert,
150			// so the two must not both be set (mutually exclusive, fail-fast).
151			if c.TargetResidual < 0 || c.TargetResidual >= 1 {
152				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be in (0,1) when set, got %g", c.TargetResidual)
153			}
154			if c.SafetyFactor != 0 {
155				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor and TargetResidual are mutually exclusive (TargetResidual is the primary residual-SLA surface); leave SafetyFactor 0 when TargetResidual is set, got SafetyFactor=%g", c.SafetyFactor)
156			}
157		} else if math.IsNaN(c.SafetyFactor) || math.IsInf(c.SafetyFactor, 0) || c.SafetyFactor < 1 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
```

```go
704	// session id never conflates two peers' probe streams, and a runtime-added path (T30) reuses it
705	// so its probes join this boot's stream and the peer's reflector adopts them without a
706	// challenge reset.
707	func buildScheduler(cfg *config.Config, psk config.Key, sessionID uint64, lg log.Logger) (sched.Scheduler, []*telemetry.Prober, bind.ProberFactory, error) {
708		clock := telemetry.SystemClock{}
709		proberCfg := telemetry.ProberConfig{
710			LossWindow: telemetry.DefaultLossWindow,
711			Liveness: telemetry.LivenessConfig{
712				DownAfter:        telemetry.DefaultDownAfter,
713				UpAfterSuccesses: telemetry.DefaultUpSuccesses,
714			},
715		}
716		// newProber mints one path's Prober with the shared session/config/clock, keyed on THIS
717		// peer's psk. It is the single construction point for this peer's boot-time AND runtime
718		// paths, so both measure liveness identically.
719		newProber := func(name string, id uint8) *telemetry.Prober {
720			return telemetry.NewProber(name, id, sessionID, psk, proberCfg, clock, lg)
721		}
722		probers := make([]*telemetry.Prober, len(cfg.Paths))
723		health := make([]sched.PathHealth, len(cfg.Paths))
724		quality := make([]sched.PathQuality, len(cfg.Paths))
725		for i := range cfg.Paths {
726			probers[i] = newProber(cfg.Paths[i].Name, uint8(i))
727			health[i] = probers[i]
728			quality[i] = probers[i]
729		}
730		// Policy is a config choice: active-backup (P1, default) or the T21 weighted-
731		// aggregation policy. Both consume the SAME per-path *Prober set — a *Prober
732		// satisfies BOTH PathHealth (liveness) and PathQuality (RTT/loss Estimate) — so the
733		// probe transport drives the very liveness/quality the scheduler selects on, and the
734		// swap is behind config with no Bind change.
735		scheduler, err := selectScheduler(cfg, health, quality, clock, lg)
736		if err != nil {
737			return nil, nil, nil, err
738		}
739		return scheduler, probers, newProber, nil
740	}
741	
742	// selectScheduler builds the send scheduler the configured policy names, over the
743	// per-path health (and, for the weighted policy, quality) sources. active-backup is
744	// the P1 default; weighted is T21. The weighted knobs are validated at config load,
745	// so translating them here cannot fail on range — only NewWeighted's structural
746	// checks (which the wiring satisfies) apply.
747	func selectScheduler(cfg *config.Config, health []sched.PathHealth, quality []sched.PathQuality, clock telemetry.Clock, lg log.Logger) (sched.Scheduler, error) {
748		switch cfg.Scheduler.Policy {
749		case config.PolicyWeighted:
750			sc := cfg.Scheduler
751			return sched.NewWeighted(health, quality, sched.WeightedConfig{
752				PerPathCapacity:   sc.PerPathCapacityFPS,
753				EngageFraction:    sc.EngageFraction,
754				DisengageFraction: sc.DisengageFraction,
755				CollapseDwell:     sc.CollapseDwell,
756				LoadTau:           sc.LoadTau,
757				Pacing:            sc.PacingEnabled,
758				PacingBurst:       sc.PacingBurstFrames,
759				WeightRTTFloor:    sc.WeightRTTFloor,
760				WeightLossFloor:   sc.WeightLossFloor,
761			}, clock, lg)
762		default:
763			// active-backup (and the empty default, normalized to it at config load).
764			return sched.NewActiveBackup(
765				health,
766				sched.Config{FailbackAfter: defaultFailbackDwell},
767				clock,
768				lg,
769			)
770		}
771	}

776	// Wait blocks until the device is torn down (its own Close, or an unrecoverable
777	// engine error).
778	func (t *Tunnel) Wait() { <-t.dev.Wait() }

780	// Close stops the probe loop, brings the device down, and releases the TUN and
781	// Bind. Idempotent. It also releases this tunnel's hold on the single-amnezia-
782	// engine-per-process guard exactly once, so a later Up may reconfigure the
783	// process-global amnezia state. Probing is stopped BEFORE the engine tears the
784	// bind's sockets down so no emission races the close.
785	func (t *Tunnel) Close() {
786		// Shut the scrape endpoint FIRST so no in-flight /metrics scrape reads the Bind while
787		// the engine tears its sockets down. reloadMu serializes this against a concurrent
788		// SIGHUP-driven rebind.
789		t.reloadMu.Lock()
790		t.stopMetricsLocked()
791		t.reloadMu.Unlock()
792		if t.stopProbes != nil {
793			t.stopProbes()
794		}
795		if t.stopReconcile != nil {
796			t.stopReconcile()
797		}
798		if t.stopHubFailover != nil {
799			t.stopHubFailover()
800		}
801		// Stop the DNS re-resolution loop between hub-failover and the engine teardown (T74): no
802		// re-resolve may race the engine peer's install/repoint or the socket close.
803		if t.stopResolution != nil {
804			t.stopResolution()
805		}
806		// Stop the WG-session monitor before the engine teardown (I2): no IpcGet may race the
807		// engine's Close.
808		if t.stopSession != nil {
809			t.stopSession()
810		}
811		t.dev.Close()
812		t.releaseOnce.Do(func() { globalAmneziaGuard.release(t.amnezia) })
813	}
```

```go
772	// Configured reports whether the amnezia block carries any obfuscation parameter.
773	// An all-zero block leaves the engine in plain WireGuard mode.
774	func (a Amnezia) Configured() bool {
775		return a.Jc != 0 || a.Jmin != 0 || a.Jmax != 0 || a.S1 != 0 || a.S2 != 0 ||
776			a.H1 != 0 || a.H2 != 0 || a.H3 != 0 || a.H4 != 0
777	}
778	
779	// applyDefaults fills in the standard magic headers (1..4) when the block is
780	// configured but no header was given, so the UAPI renderer emits an explicit,
781	// complete header set instead of h1=0..h4=0. It is a no-op for an unconfigured
782	// block and for one that already sets any header (a partial header set is left
783	// intact so validate can reject it).
784	func (a *Amnezia) applyDefaults() {
785		if !a.Configured() {
786			return
787		}
788		if a.H1 == 0 && a.H2 == 0 && a.H3 == 0 && a.H4 == 0 {
789			a.H1, a.H2, a.H3, a.H4 = defaultMagicHeaders[0], defaultMagicHeaders[1], defaultMagicHeaders[2], defaultMagicHeaders[3]
790		}
791	}
```

```go
1	package adaptivefec
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"math"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// MaxShards mirrors the Reed-Solomon field limit enforced in internal/fec
10	// (maxShards) and restated in internal/config (maxFECShards): a coding group
11	// carries at most 256 shards (data + parity) total over GF(2^8). It is mirrored
12	// here — with this cross-reference rather than an import — so the controller can
13	// fail-fast on an out-of-field parity bound without coupling to the datapath.
14	const MaxShards = 256
15	
16	// Documented default control-law constants. They are the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
17	// for the tuning the simulation harness exercises; DefaultConfig assembles them.
18	// Every value is named and justified rather than appearing as a magic number in
19	// the algorithm.
20	const (
21		// DefaultDataShards is K, the fixed number of data shards per coding group the
22		// controller sizes parity for. Chosen to match a typical FEC group.
23		DefaultDataShards = 10
24	
25		// DefaultMaxParity caps M at 100% overhead (M == K): beyond a 1:1 parity ratio
26		// the marginal recovery per byte of overhead is poor, so the loop saturates
27		// there rather than spending unbounded bandwidth under pathological loss.
28		DefaultMaxParity = 10
29	
30		// DefaultAlpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample. 0.3 keeps ~3-4
31		// samples of memory: heavy enough to reject single-sample telemetry noise, light
32		// enough to react to a genuine sustained shift within a few probe intervals.
33		DefaultAlpha = 0.3
34	
35		// DefaultRaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (5%) at/above which the loop
36		// starts adding parity. Below it, occasional loss is cheaper to absorb via
37		// retransmit/latency than to mask with standing redundancy.
38		DefaultRaiseThreshold = 0.05
39	
40		// DefaultLowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level (2%) at/below which the loop
41		// is eligible to shed parity. The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis
42		// deadband: loss wandering between 2% and 5% neither raises nor lowers M.
43		DefaultLowerThreshold = 0.02
44	
45		// DefaultSafetyFactor is the headroom over the mean loss the parity is sized to
46		// mask. 1.5 sizes each group to tolerate 1.5x the smoothed loss, covering the
47		// binomial variance by which an individual group exceeds the mean. It applies
48		// only when TargetResidual is unset (the legacy SafetyFactor sizing mode).
49		DefaultSafetyFactor = 1.5
50	
51		// DefaultMaxStep bounds |ΔM| per RateInterval. A single spike can move M by at
52		// most 2 parity shards per interval, so redundancy slews rather than jumps.
53		DefaultMaxStep = 2
54	
55		// DefaultRateInterval is the minimum wall time between two M changes. Combined
56		// with MaxStep it caps the redundancy slew rate at MaxStep shards per 500ms.
57		DefaultRateInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
58	
59		// DefaultDwell is how long smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold before
60		// the first decrease. 3s of quiet is required to shed parity, so a brief lull in
61		// a lossy period does not prematurely strip protection (raise fast, lower slow).
62		DefaultDwell = 3 * time.Second
63	)
64	
65	// Config parameterizes the adaptive FEC controller. All fields are validated at
66	// construction (fail-fast): a mis-tuned band or an out-of-field parity bound is
67	// rejected by NewController rather than producing undefined control behavior at
68	// runtime.
69	type Config struct {
70		// DataShards is K, the fixed group size the controller sizes parity for. >= 1.
71		DataShards int
72		// MaxParity is the upper bound on the emitted M. >= 1 and DataShards+MaxParity
73		// <= MaxShards (the Reed-Solomon field limit).
74		MaxParity int
75		// Alpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample, in (0,1]. Smaller = more
76		// smoothing.
77		Alpha float64
78		// RaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/above which M may rise. In
79		// (LowerThreshold, 1).
80		RaiseThreshold float64
81		// LowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/below which M may fall. In
82		// [0, RaiseThreshold). The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis deadband.
83		LowerThreshold float64
84		// SafetyFactor is the headroom multiplier on the smoothed loss the parity is
85		// sized to mask. >= 1 in the SafetyFactor sizing mode (TargetResidual unset);
86		// MUST be 0 when TargetResidual is set (the two modes are mutually exclusive).
87		SafetyFactor float64
88		// TargetResidual, when set (> 0), selects the RESIDUAL-SLA sizing mode and is
89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)
94		// when set; 0 selects the legacy SafetyFactor path.
95		TargetResidual float64
96		// MaxStep bounds |ΔM| applied per RateInterval. >= 1.
97		MaxStep int
98		// RateInterval is the minimum time between two M changes. > 0.
99		RateInterval time.Duration
100		// Dwell is the minimum time smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold
101		// before the first decrease. >= 0.
102		Dwell time.Duration
103	}
104	
105	// DefaultConfig returns a Config populated from the documented default control
106	// constants.
107	func DefaultConfig() Config {
108		return Config{
109			DataShards:     DefaultDataShards,
110			MaxParity:      DefaultMaxParity,
111			Alpha:          DefaultAlpha,
112			RaiseThreshold: DefaultRaiseThreshold,
113			LowerThreshold: DefaultLowerThreshold,
114			SafetyFactor:   DefaultSafetyFactor,
115			MaxStep:        DefaultMaxStep,
116			RateInterval:   DefaultRateInterval,
117			Dwell:          DefaultDwell,
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Validate enforces the control-law invariants, failing fast so a mis-tuned
122	// controller is rejected at construction rather than misbehaving at runtime.
123	func (c Config) Validate() error {
124		if c.DataShards < 1 {
125			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards (K) must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
126		}
127		if c.MaxParity < 1 {
128			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxParity must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxParity)
129		}
130		if c.DataShards+c.MaxParity > MaxShards {
131			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards+MaxParity must be <= %d (Reed-Solomon field limit), got %d", MaxShards, c.DataShards+c.MaxParity)
132		}
133		if !(c.Alpha > 0 && c.Alpha <= 1) || math.IsNaN(c.Alpha) {
134			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Alpha must be in (0,1], got %g", c.Alpha)
135		}
136		if math.IsNaN(c.LowerThreshold) || c.LowerThreshold < 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold must be >= 0, got %g", c.LowerThreshold)
138		}
139		if math.IsNaN(c.RaiseThreshold) || c.RaiseThreshold >= 1 {
140			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RaiseThreshold must be < 1, got %g", c.RaiseThreshold)
141		}
142		if c.LowerThreshold >= c.RaiseThreshold {
143			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold (%g) must be < RaiseThreshold (%g) so the hysteresis deadband is non-empty", c.LowerThreshold, c.RaiseThreshold)
144		}
145		if math.IsNaN(c.TargetResidual) {
146			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be a number, got NaN")
147		}
148		if c.TargetResidual != 0 {
149			// Residual-SLA sizing mode: TargetResidual governs and SafetyFactor is inert,
150			// so the two must not both be set (mutually exclusive, fail-fast).
151			if c.TargetResidual < 0 || c.TargetResidual >= 1 {
152				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be in (0,1) when set, got %g", c.TargetResidual)
153			}
154			if c.SafetyFactor != 0 {
155				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor and TargetResidual are mutually exclusive (TargetResidual is the primary residual-SLA surface); leave SafetyFactor 0 when TargetResidual is set, got SafetyFactor=%g", c.SafetyFactor)
156			}
157		} else if math.IsNaN(c.SafetyFactor) || math.IsInf(c.SafetyFactor, 0) || c.SafetyFactor < 1 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
```

```go
303	// Encode appends the wire encoding of f to dst and returns the extended slice,
304	// letting the caller reuse one buffer across sends (pass dst[:0]). It fails only
305	// if the system CSPRNG is unavailable.
306	func (c *Codec) Encode(dst []byte, f Frame) ([]byte, error) {
307		// Build the plaintext body (kind || header || payload) in reusable scratch.
308		c.encScratch = f.appendBody(c.encScratch[:0])
309		body := c.encScratch
310	
311		start := len(dst)
312		dst = append(dst, make([]byte, nonceLen)...)
313		nonce := dst[start : start+nonceLen]
314		if _, err := rand.Read(nonce); err != nil {
315			return nil, fmt.Errorf("frame: read nonce: %w", err)
316		}
317	
318		// Obfuscate the body in scratch, then append it; a single keystream init.
319		obfuscate(c.obfKey, nonce, body)
320		dst = append(dst, body...)
321	
322		if f.Kind().authenticated() {
323			// After the append above, nonce and obfBody are contiguous in dst's
324			// (possibly reallocated) backing array; tag over nonce||obfBody.
325			nonce = dst[start : start+nonceLen]
326			obfBody := dst[start+nonceLen:]
327			dst = append(dst, tag(c.authKey, nonce, obfBody)...)
328		}
329		return dst, nil
330	}
```

19	- **[docs/install.md](docs/install.md)** — setup and operation. Update when

29	
30	## Build, test, lint

32	The dev shell (`nix develop`) puts Go 1.26, golangci-lint, and the netem/DPI
33	tooling on `PATH`. Run tools from it (e.g. `nix develop --command bash -c '…'`).
34

36	just build      # go build ./...
37	just test       # unprivileged unit/property tests (go test ./...)
38	just lint       # go vet + golangci-lint, INCLUDING -tags e2e and -tags realhosts
39	just e2e        # privileged netns fixture: sudo -E go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...
40	just realhosts  # opt-in real-machine tier (-tags realhosts), report-only
41	```
42

44	
45	```sh
46	go build ./... && go vet ./... && test -z "$(gofmt -l cmd internal test)" && go test ./...
47	```
48	
49	`-tags e2e` / `-tags realhosts` need root / real hosts and are **not** part of the
50	default gate; validate them separately (see Testing discipline).
51	
52	## Load-bearing invariants — do not break

55	short:

57	1. The engine sees **one virtual endpoint per peer**; the Bind fans out beneath
58	   it (design rule A1). No per-packet endpoint churn to the engine.
59	2. Use wanbond's **own outer sequence space**; never reuse/perturb the inner
60	   WireGuard counter.
61	3. **Resequence before** the inner anti-replay window validates.
62	4. Inner fail-closed; **PROBE/CONTROL are PSK-HMAC authenticated** with monotonic
63	   anti-replay; DATA/PARITY are unauthenticated **by design** (DoS-grade risk
64	   accepted — do not "fix" this without a design decision).
65	5. All engine (`conn`) coupling stays isolated to **`internal/bind/bind.go`** —
66	   preserve the fork-swap hedge.
67	6. Amnezia config is **all-or-nothing** and the engine is **single-per-process**
68	   (package-level globals). Keep the config validation that enforces this.
69	7. On **any `klauspost/reedsolomon` (or amneziawg-go) version bump**, re-verify
70	   `TestKlauspostParityPrefixStableInvariant` (`internal/fec`) before landing —

81	  failover, FEC recovery, and DPI — **not** absolute throughput or bufferbloat.
